prose:
Fukui reactivity analysis of formaldehyde was computed using DFT at the B3LYP/def2-TZVP level (tier 'standard') in the gas phase with PySCF, using exact four-center integrals (no density fitting). Geometry: input .xyz file (4 atoms) used as-is, no geometry optimization. Charge 0, multiplicity 1 (neutral singlet); cation and anion states computed at multiplicity 2 (doublets). Mulliken partial charges were computed for all three electronic states, and Fukui indices (f⁺, f⁻, f⁰) were derived from the charge differences between neutral and perturbed (N±1) states. All SCF calculations converged successfully. The most electrophilic site is the carbon atom with f⁺ = 0.7083913, indicating high susceptibility to nucleophilic attack. The most nucleophilic site is the oxygen atom with f⁻ = 0.46742464. The dual descriptor δf is largest at carbon (0.59594709), confirming it as the preferential nucleophilic attack site. Charge-conservation checks passed (sum of f⁺ and f⁻ both equal ~1.0). Calculation integrity: status ok, trustworthy true. No warnings reported beyond routine convergence checks.</prose>
<parameter name="n_atoms">4
